entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Service...
entry.content:
Good day @bangaru198251 

Thank you for your question.

First let’s look at what solutions the Public Cloud offer for your various stories before I address the question directly.

User Story 1: Complex Discount Setup

In standard Professional Services, the project planning model supports planned cost and planned revenue on work packages and billing is handled through the project billing/sales side. In service based work packages, planned revenue is determined from the price of the service and planned cost from planned resources. I am not aware of any functionality in the Public Cloud that creates separate project planning buckets such as Planned Discount and Negotiated Discount as independent discount categories with their own project level accounting logic.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/b17342af91d74c58b7b2afc74d6b3dd5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/b2a4b6024aa54e30af5a523ff62f43bc.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/02fd3a7a00f244a68768570f8c73e5df.html?locale=en-US

So, the answer for User Story 1 is: It is not supported as you described. Standard supports:

  • Planned cost and planned revenue in project planning
  • Pricing and discount conditions on the billing/sales side

Standard does not provide a dedicated Professional Services project feature to manage multiple named discount categories as separate planning object at project, work package, or customer level with build in validation against a contractual amount.

User Story 2:  Revenue Recognition with Discount Consideration

For Projects with Event Based Revenue Recognition, the app displays planned revenue and planned cost based on plan category REVREC01 and SAP’s cost based POC method uses the formula recognised revenue = POC x planned revenue. So, for EBRR customer projects, planned costs come from resource planning and planned revenue.

So, if your values are Planned Revenue = 25,000 and Contractual Amount = 21,500, then standard EBRR will not independently switch to 21,500 while leaving planned revenue at 25,000. If EBRR must recognise revenue against 21,500, then the revenue plan used by EBRR, meaning REVREC01 planned revenue, must align with 21,500.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/1ab3aa92c5df43a9aca1cc2ed437a375.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/dd88281cfa3541118f3674b95d0f584c.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/ff4cdf3071684039b4ebc14074ca6cbe.html?locale=en-US

The cleanest Public Cloud design is to separate the business concepts like this:

  • Planned Costs stay in project planning
  • EBRR revenue plan should reflect the amount you want recognised, so in your case 21,500
  • Billing/contractual discounts should be handled on the sales pricing side
  • Any extra analytical distinction such as Planned Discount= 1,300 and Negotiated Discount= 1,200 should be tracked through custom fields plus reporting, not through standard EBRR logic.

This is because standard project EBRR is driven by the revenue plan used in REVREC01, while pricing conditions and discounts belong to the billing/sales side. Custom fields and custom CDS views are the standard extensibility approach in Public Cloud when you need additional reporting dimensions.

See the following references for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b0000072fe994d7b9d5e015adf066059.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/55a7cb346519450cb9e6d21c1ecd6ec1/20a91308a4304cc297e2f2e36198c616.html?locale=en-US

So, to directly answer your questions:

User Story 1

Goal: I want to configure multiple discount types at the project level
So that I can differentiate between planned discounts and negotiated discounts while maintaining accurate financial planning.

Answer: No, not as described. Standard supports pricing and discount on the sales side but not separate native project discount categories with independent EBBR behaviour. The usual Public Cloud approach is to use custom fields for discount type reporting and Custom CDS View or released CDS views for analytics.

User Story 2

Goal: As a Project Controller: I want Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) to consider the contractual amount ($21,500) rather than planned revenue ($25,000)
So that revenue recognition reflects actual customer commitments and discount impacts

Answer: No, not in standard EBRR project. Standard EBRR for projects uses planned revenue from REVREC01. If you want recognised revenue based on 21,500 then the EBRR relevant planned revenue must be 21,500.

In summary:

  • Standard project planning supports planned costs and planned revenue, while discounts are mainly handled on the sales and billing side, not as separate native project discount categories.
  • Even-Based Revenue Recognition uses the planned revenue in REVREC01, so if revenue recognition should be based on 21,500 then the EBRR relevant planned revenue must also be 21,500, not 25,00.
  • The recommended design is to use 21,500 as the contractual revenue for EBRR, keep 25,000 as a separate commercial target analysis, and store planned discount and negotiated discount in custom fields.
  • Use custom CDS views to show the full picture clearly.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning Hub subscription activation
entry.content:
Hello,

Regarding the delay in activating my SAP Learning Hub subscription after renewal, and share the resolution — hoping this helps others who may face the same issue.  After raising cases with the SAP Learning team and SAP Store, I realized the activation was not a backend issue at all. For subscription renewals, the license assignment needs to be done manually by the user themselves.

Follow these simple steps to assign your license and activate access:

1. Log in to SAP My Learning ->Go to My Profile -> Navigate to User and License Management-> Assign your Email ID and S-User ID
After few minutes ,Your subscription access will be activated!

This step is easy to miss during the renewal process, as it is not prominently highlighted. If your subscription is not activated after renewal payment, please check this before raising a support ticket — it will save you time and effort!

Regards,

RenugaA

entry.title: Re: Is there a character limit for a field in CrystalRepo...
entry.content: When the data source is a stored procedure, Crystal Reports recognizes the default data type. If the field in the stored procedure is the result of a string operation, Crystal Reports recognizes it as a string by default and assigns a maximum of 5000 characters. If you have a text field, it's best to display it directly and perform any operation with this field in Crystal Reports; this way, Crystal Reports recognizes the field as Long Text.

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: Popular Blog Posts, ABAP Certification Updates, New Location Groups and More!
entry.content:
We're excited to bring you another edition filled with highlights and updates from across our global SAP Community.

From deep-dive technical blog posts on topics like Agentic AI and Cloud Integration alerts to the latest news around the ABAP Cloud certification and a recap of UI5con Bengaluru 2026. We're also happy to welcome three new SAP Community Location Groups: Chisinau, Linz, and Mexico! You will also find upcoming community events (including SAP Inside Track Madrid!) so you can stay engaged with what's happening across the SAP ecosystem.

Let's dive in!

Featured Blog Posts 

Agentic Reporting: AI-Driven Insights 

Most enterprise dashboards still focus on reporting what happened, leaving analysts to interpret whether KPI changes matter. In her recent blog post series, the SAP Community member Shruti Srivastava introduces the concept of Agentic Reporting, where AI-powered agents continuously observe data, understand context, and surface only the deviations that truly require attention. Framed within the SAP analytics landscape, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Business Technology Platform, the post explains how agentic systems can move beyond rigid thresholds and static alerts by considering factors such as seasonality, historical patterns, and business context before raising an issue. Using a KPI breach monitoring example, it shows how the same metric change could either be ignored or escalated depending on contextual signals, with the agent providing root-cause insights and recommended actions. With clear comparisons and visuals illustrating the concept, we recommend reading for anyone interested in how AI and technologies like Joule could transform reporting from passive dashboards into proactive decision support. 

Read Shruti blog post here 

What's New in Asset Management in SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 

SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 introduces a wide range of enhancements across Asset Management, improving maintenance execution, safety compliance, planning, and technical object management. In this detailed blog post, the SAP expert Fernanda Rodrigues provides a curated overview of the most important innovations, including embedded Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) isolation management within permit-to-work processes, enhancements to maintenance orders and inspection checklists, improved handling of unplanned work confirmations, and usability upgrades in the Perform Maintenance Jobs app. The article also highlights planning improvements such as expanded maintenance plan capabilities, more flexible planning buckets, and better analytics for phase-based maintenance, along with master data innovations like unified technical object search and new reference equipment and structure management. With screenshots and practical explanations throughout, this post is a helpful read for maintenance technicians, planners, and reliability engineers who want to understand the latest capabilities and process improvements. 

Read Fernanda blog post here 

Setting Up Alerts for Failed iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration 

Monitoring integration failures is critical for reliable operations, and to support on automated alerts for failed iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration using SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP, SAP Community member  Nikhil_Gursal provides a practical step-by-step guide showing how to create the Alert Notification service instance in SAP Business Technology Platform. He shows how to configure notification channels such as email, define event-based subscriptions for integration flow failures, and set up the standard alert notification iFlow that retrieves failed message logs and forwards them to the alert service. With screenshots covering configuration, service keys, OAuth credential setup, and testing scenarios, this blog post offers a clear and simple tips for integration developers who want to ensure they receive timely notifications whenever an iFlow fails. 

Read Nikhil blog post here 

Understanding Agentic Memory in AI Systems 

Agentic memory is becoming an important concept in modern AI systems and agent frameworks, enabling intelligent agents to "remember" past interactions and use that knowledge to improve future decisions. In this blog post, the SAP expert Lennart Adenaw explores what Agentic Memory actually means, when it should be used, and how it differs from traditional approaches like databases or retrieval systems. Using a first-principles perspective, the article explains which types of information are suitable for agentic memory, such as emergent insights, personalization signals, or lessons learned from feedback, and which are better handled through APIs, databases, or retrieval methods. The post also outlines key business use cases including personalization, contextual collaboration across sessions, multi-agent coordination, and agent self-improvement, while proposing a practical definition of agentic memory as a lifecycle system that captures, evolves, and retrieves knowledge derived from interactions. For everyone exploring AI agents and intelligent automation, this is a thoughtful introduction to how memory can enable more adaptive and effective agent behavior. 

Read Lennart blog post here 

Community News 

SAP’s Most Popular Certification Gets an Upgrade 

SAP’s popular certification for back-end developers, the Back-End Developer ABAP Cloud exam, is getting a huge upgrade! March also marks the SAP Community developer challenge for ABAP start, making this the perfect moment to sharpen your skills and validate your expertise. SAP expert Arabella, recently published a blog post on how the updated certification for ABAP developers moves beyond traditional multiple-choice exams to a hands-on, open-book format where candidates complete time-boxed tasks directly in live SAP systems. Built around realistic scenarios and AI-supported simulations, the exam allows access to learning resources such as the SAP Help Portal, SAP Community, and tools like Joule while emphasizing practical skills rather than memorization. She also highlights recommended learning paths, live learning sessions, and hands-on opportunities like SAP CodeJam events to help developers prepare. With the certification experience now aligned more closely with real project work, this is the perfect moment for community members to take advantage of the new format and not miss the chance to get certified and showcase their ABAP Cloud expertise. Make sure to check the entire content to find more valuable links to support you on your learning journey as well.  

Read Arabella blog post here 

New Location Groups! 

The end of February and beginning of March marked new milestones for our Location Groups! Now, if you are located in Chisinau, Linz or Mexico, you can join your local community group inside SAP Community and connect with members in your area. This marks a great outcome in our goal to keep growing the SAP community all over the world and bringing light to the great experts and learners in different countries. Through the location groups, you are able to share knowledge in your preferred language, build connections, and plan on-site meetups. And to keep everything moving, we would like to highlight our amazing new group curators Edgar, Maria Antonieta e Sandra (Mexico), Marcus (Linz), Natalia and Tsovinar (Chisinau)! 

Get to Know our SAP Mentors 

The SAP Mentors is a program that has been growing and bringing opportunities for SAP experts since 2007. And we are happy to acknowledge how much the program has grown and created valuable changes on behalf of our SAP ecosystem. Today, we would like to bring light to one of our oldest members, Gregor Wolf, SAP Mentor since 2007. Gregor is an independent SAP solution architect and developer, focused on SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), Node.JS, SAP Fiori Elements and Security. He has supported several customers during their SAP Cloud ERP Private Edition (S/4HANA On Premise) implementation. Since the end of 2024, he is currently also supporting technical topics at an SAP Cloud ERP Public Edition Customer. SAP Advocate Stephanie Marley published a recent blog post, sharing details on why Gregor decided to become an SAP Mentor and what inspires him to be part of the community. Make sure to read it and give Gregor your support in the comments. We are more than grateful for having him and many others bringing expertise, sharing knowledge, and putting so much effort into our community!  

Read Stephanie blog post here 

Events 

UI5con Bengaluru 2026 Recap 

After a seven-year pause, UI5con returned to India with UI5con Bengaluru 2026, bringing together more than 400 developers, architects, consultants, and students for a full day of learning and community collaboration at SAP Labs. Freddy Jetty Johnson published a great recap blog post, highlighting key moments like the opening keynote exploring agentic coding and AI-assisted development with SAPUI5, to deep dives into modern UX development with SAP Fiori and new capabilities such as UI5 Web Components 2.0. With live demos, developer labs, and an interactive “Ask the Expert” panel featuring core UI5 experts, the event highlighted how AI, developer tooling, and community collaboration are shaping the future of UI development. 

SAP Inside Track Madrid 

We are happy to announce the SAP Inside Track Madrid 2026 on April 17, 2026, bringing together community members for a day of knowledge sharing, networking, and SAP discussions at the NEORIS Artificial Intelligence Hub. This community-driven event is the perfect place to learn from fellow experts, exchange ideas, and connect with other SAP professionals in an informal and collaborative setting. With space limited to just 70 attendees, spots are expected to fill quickly, so make sure to RSVP early to secure your place in the event page at SAP Community here. Also, if you would like to share your expertise, speaker proposals are also welcome, making this a wonderful opportunity to contribute to the community. Submit your proposal here.  

The SAP Intelligence Event in Amsterdam 

This week is happening the SAP Intelligence Event in Amsterdam, and many of our SAP Community Advocates are taking part to share more about their expertise and connect with local developers and SAP community members. This whole week (until March 11), the event is creating the perfect opportunity to understand more about AI Experience for SAP Developers, and we are also happy to have some of our SAP Mentors as speakers: Marla Schweiki, Robin Van Het Hof and Wouter Lemaire. In case you are participating, don´t forget to share more about your experience in our Community Corner! 

SAPinsider Las Vegas 2026 

Next week another great SAP event is going to take place in Las Vegas, Nevada, the SAPinsider 2026! From March 16 to 19, more than 2.000 SAP professionals are gathering to learn from 200+ experts across sessions, to understand what it is working and what needs to be improved. This is also a good opportunity to connect with local experts, get a better view on real-world strategies, customer case studies, and connect with SAP practitioners, architects, and business leaders. The Head of our SAP Community, Josh “Bluebeard” Bentley, will be present at the event and will bring a great recap for our Community Voice in the next few weeks!

entry.title: Planned Order Showing External Procurement Even Though Procurement Type = E (SAP PP)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP PP where the system determines external procurement even though the material is configured for in-house production.

  • Material: 5583

  • Plant: 0001

  • Material Type: FERT

  • MRP Type: PD

  • Procurement Type: E (In-house)

  • Special Procurement: Blank

After running MRP, the system creates a planned order, but in MD04 the planned order shows:

Procurement Indicator = F (External Procurement)

When I try to convert the planned order into a production order, the system shows the message:

“Material 5583 0001 is to be procured externally.” 

Checks done :

  • BOM exists and is valid.

  • Routing exists and Status = 4 (Released).

  • Production Version maintained with correct validity and lot size.

  • MRP re-run after all corrections.

  • Checked table MARC → BESKZ = E, SOBSL = Blank.

What could cause MRP to determine external procurement (F) even though the material has Procurement Type = E and no special procurement key?

Could this be due to MRP configuration or procurement determination logic overriding the material master?

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: what are the alternative options for VBS scripts since it will be deprecated by Microsoft?
entry.content:
I'm contacting you to confirm the future direction of SAP Scripting (VBS-based), an automation feature for SAP.

As Microsoft has indicated its intention to gradually deprecate VBS,

I would like to confirm whether SAP Scripting can continue to be used, or whether it is scheduled for deprecation.

If it is going to be deprecated, I would also appreciate it if you could also let me know:

- Are there any alternative automation methods (officially recommended tools)?

- If so, what are their overviews?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: what are the alternative options for VBS scripts sinc...
entry.content:
Hello customer,

Indeed there is a VBScript deprecation timeline from Microsoft as outlined in the VBScript deprecation: Timelines and next steps | Windows IT Pro Blog . As you can see there, the "Feature removal from OS" is To Be Defined (TBD).

The implications of the future VBScript deprecation in the SAP Scripting is explained in the SAP Note  3484031 - "Deprecation of VBScript by Microsoft - impact on SAP GUI Scripting"."

"At the moment VBScript is still part of Microsoft Windows and there is no concrete timeline for removing the feature completely. Should VBScript be missing in future versions of Windows it will for a limited period of time still be possible to activate it (see also https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/whats-new/deprecated-features). Thus, it is our recommendation to make use of this activation as long as possible, because only if VBScript is available, you will be facing no impact on the usage of SAP GUI Scripting in SAP GUI for Windows. You can use the time to prepare by migrating any scripts recorded via SAP GUI Scripting using VBScript to another language "

Fortunately, in the upcoming SAP GUI for Windows 8.10 release, an alternative scripting language, JScript, is supported for script replaying, alongside VBScript, to ensure compatibility with SAP GUI for Java. 

As VBScript may become obsolete, SAP has enabled JScript recording in the latest release. The type of script recording, JScript or VBScript, is determined by the file extension used during saving: ".js" for JScript and ".vbs" for VBScript. Currently, there are no plans to support additional scripting languages like PowerShell, though this could change based on demand.

To prepare for a potential phase-out of VBScript, it’s advisable to manually migrate existing scripts to JScript. While automatic conversion isn’t supported, the transition is relatively straightforward due to the similar syntax between VBScript and JScript.

I hope I was able to clarify your questions and concerns.


 
Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Creating a DQM Validation Rule for Business Partner Role Assignment in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Overview

Data quality is critical in Master Data Governance (MDG), especially when dealing with Business Partners. One common data quality issue occurs when Business Partners are created without any Business Partner Role assigned. Since roles determine how a Business Partner participates in business processes (customer, supplier, etc.), missing roles can break downstream applications.

The goal is to create a DQM validation rule in SAP Master Data Governance that prevents the creation or modification of Business Partners without role assignments. The system will show a validation error or warning as shown in the following screenshot:

The validation logic works as follows:

  1. The rule iterates over Business Partner records in table BUT000.
  2. For each Business Partner ID, a table operation checks table BUT100.
  3. If no entry exists in BUT100 for that BP, the validation rule raises an error.

This ensures that every Business Partner has at least one role assigned before it can be saved.

Prerequisites

Before implementing the rule, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Master Data Governance with Data Quality Management (DQM) enabled
  • User authorization with the SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_STEWARD role or equivalent
  • Access to the SAP Fiori application Validation Rules for Business Partners (App IDs F4084 or F4082A (newer releases)

Rule Configuration

Step 1 — Access the DQM Validation App

  1. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Launch the Validation Rules for Business Partners app.

This UI/App allows you to manage and create validation rules for Business Partner data

Step 2 — Create a New Validation Rule

Click Create and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Rule ID

CHECK_BP_ROLE_MANDATORY

Rule Name

BP Role Assignment Required

Base Table

BUT000 (Business Partner General Data)

Checked Field

BUT0BK-PARTNER_ROLE (optional but recommended)

The base table must be BUT000, even though the validation checks data stored in another table

Step 3 — Maintain General Information

Fill in the business metadata for the rule:

Description
Ensures every Business Partner has at least one role assigned.

Reason
Business Partners without roles cannot participate in business processes.

Rule Owner
Data Steward responsible for Business Partner data.

Implementation Expert
Technical contact responsible for BRF+ implementation.

Save the rule by clicking Create.

Step 4 — Technical Implementation

Configure Rule Usage

  1. In the Usage section, select Add.
  2. Choose Check in Central Governance.
  3. Click Prepare to generate the BRF+ application.
  4. Select Send for Implementation.

This enables the rule logic to be edited.

Scope Expression Configuration

The Scope Expression defines which Business Partners should be validated.

Steps:

  1. Open the Scope Expression link.
  2. The Simplified BRF+ Workbench appears.
  3. Define the scope.

Example scope:

Partner Category = 2 (Organization)

This means the validation rule will only apply to organizational Business Partners.

You can also configure the rule to apply to all Business Partners.

Condition Expression Implementation

The main validation logic is defined in the Condition Expression.

Steps:

  1. Open the Condition Expression.
  2. Remove the dummy condition.
  3. Create a new expression.

Choose:

Expression Type:
Table Operation

Example name:

BUT100_CHECK

Table Operation Configuration

Configure the operation as follows:

Parameter

Value

Operation

Has at least

Table

Procedure Call

Procedure

BUT0BK lookup ALL

Condition

Partner Role is not initial

This configuration checks if at least one role exists for the Business Partner.

 

Although the rule loops over BUT000, the lookup ultimately checks role entries in BUT100.

This cross-table validation is the key technical detail of this solution.

Activation and Deployment

Once testing is complete:

  1. Select Send for Testing to change the rule status.
  2. After validation, choose Approve.
  3. Go to the Usage tab.
  4. Click Enable for Check in Central Governance.

The rule is now active and will validate Business Partner data during governance processes.

Testing the Rule

Test the validation logic.

Test Scenarios

Scenario

Expected Result

BP without roles

Validation error

BP with one role

Validation passes

BP with multiple roles

Validation passes

Testing ensures that only valid Business Partner records can be saved.

 

Troubleshooting

Rule Not Triggering

Check the following:

  • All BRF+ expressions are active
  • Rule status is Approved
  • Usage is Enabled

Performance Issues

If validation becomes slow:

  • Restrict the Scope Expression
  • Limit evaluation to specific Business Partner categories
  • Avoid unnecessary table operations

 

Best Regards

Steffen

entry.title: Re: Change Color of Edited (Unpublished) Cells in SAC Pla...
entry.content:
Unfortunately that particular CSS class is not exposed via the Story CSS, however I did create a custom widget a while back that allowed the Developer to modify the DOM of the Table at runtime. I modified the custom widget to allow the story developer to change the Modified Cell background color at runtime. 
You can access the Custom Widget here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ceq-GYaXPgp_Me_4BK8CTJieu7BUV58j?usp=sharing 
The custom widget has 4 methods exposed:

  1. setModifiedColorForAllTables(color:string) - this will change the modified color for all tables. You should be able to pass in HEX values (e.g. "#ccf4fd"), RGB values (e.g. "rgb(255, 223, 28)") or Color literals (e.g. "lightblue") as well as RGBA and HSL values should you need them.
  2. setModifiedColorForSelectedTables(tables:Table[],color:string) - this is similar to the first method but allows you to set the changes only on specific Tables on the page. In this case you would pass in an array of Table objects in addition to the color string.
  3. clearModifiedColorForAllTables() - reverts the modified color back to the default for all tables
     
  4. clearModifiedColorForSelectedTables(tables:Table[]) - reverts the modified color back to the default for the selected tables
 
Once you have the custom widget added to your tenant you can simply add it to your story (Note: custom widgets are not Global objects and must be added to each page, however as this custom widget has no front-end it adds no additional load time).
Below are some screenshots of the user experience that you can expect.
This first screenshot was taken after adding the following lines of code to my page onInitialization() event and then changing a value in the Table:
ModifiedColor_1.setModifiedColorForAllTables("#ccfdf8");
 
 
This next image is after clicking the Change Color button which has the following code:
var newColor = InputField_1.getValue();
ModifiedColor_1.setModifiedColorForAllTables(newColor);

The changes happen without refresh because we are changing the DOM itself. Finally this last screenshot was taken after clicking the Revert Color button which had the following code:

ModifiedColor_1.clearModifiedColorForAllTables();

For information on deploying Custom Widgets to your tenant check out the SAP Analytics Cloud Help guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/0ac8c6754ff84605a4372468d002f2bf/1c71e80bf2b440d4a2267a473f46ed2f.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

Sean

entry.title: Re: Synchronized scrolling for two or more tables
entry.content:
As previously mentioned, this capability is not available out of the box. However, I have created a custom widget in the past that did capture the users scrolling behavior for another use case. I updated this custom widget to support synchronized scrolling. I haven't fully tested every scenario but the basics appear to be working. 

You can download the custom widget from here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1irb8HNx1pIXUcH9bzWpKpvHE_NLSOo-S?usp=sharing 

The widget itself contains two methods:

  1. bindTables(tables:Table[],scrollLink:string) - this method takes in an array of Table objects as well as a string representing the type of scroll link that you want to bind. Valid values for scroll link are "vertical", "horizontal" and "both". When a link is established any of the linked tables can be scrolled and all of the other tables will respond accordingly. 
  2. removeAllBindings() - removes all of the bindings from all of the tables
 

Some notes that I have noticed in my brief testing using the custom widget:

  • It works best when the tables are the same size, at least in the scroll direction. I am using % in the scroll to help handle this as much as possible but it is definitely not perfect if the tables are not of similar size
  • You will need one instance of the widget for each binding. For example, if I wanted to bind Table_2 and Table_3 horizontally and Table_5 and Table_6 vertically, then I would need to add two instances of the custom widget and add code similar to the following to the page's onInitialization event:
SyncTableScroll_1.bindTables([Table_2, Table_3], "horizontal");
SyncTableScroll_2.bindTables([Table_5, Table_6], "vertical");​

Below are some screenshots that I took when testing the scrolling:
Vertical Scrolling:

Before ScrollAfter Vertical Scroll

Horizontal Scrolling:

Before ScrollAfter Horizontal Scroll

If you need instructions on how to upload a custom widget to your tenant, you can reference this section in the Help guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/0ac8c6754ff84605a4372468d002f2bf/1c71e80bf2b440d4a2267a473f46ed2f.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

Hope this helps

entry.title: Pregúntame lo que quieras con SAP Champions – Serie mensual (Sesión 2)
entry.content:
Hola querida SAP Community,

Mi nombre es María Antonieta López, SAP Champion de México.

Actualmente trabajo como Directora de Programas SAP, enfocada en programas globales de transformación SAP, implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA, arquitectura empresarial, marcos de gobernanza e innovación digital dentro del ecosistema SAP.

Como parte del equipo de SAP Champions que lidera la iniciativa “Ask Me Anything” junto con Sebastian Marchesini,

🚀yo estaré liderando la edición de marzo.

Así que sí…

Puedes preguntarme. LO QUE SEA.

 

Después de más de 20 años viviendo dentro de transformaciones SAP, probablemente he visto lo bueno, lo malo… y esos momentos de “¿cómo sobrevivió este sistema al go-live?” 😅

 

Durante las próximas semanas estaré respondiendo cualquier pregunta que tengan — sin filtros — solo SAP en vivo y real.

💡Podemos explorar temas como:

⚙️Implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA y programas globales de transformación.

🏗️ Arquitectura Empresarial para SAP utilizando enfoques basados en TOGAF.  Artefactos, ¿cómo medir la madurez, SAP Lean IX, etc...

🧼 Clean Core en SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public) — procesos de gestión de proyectos para implementar o crear extensiones o interfaces en tu proyecto.

(Hablabdo de BUILD para configuradores y su uso con Joule, APIs preconstruidas, APIs nuevas, y por supuesto nuestra plataforma como servicio SAP Integration Suite, todo dentro de nuestra querida SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)).

☁️RISE o GROW con SAP + Gobernanza y metodología Clean Core

Desde SAP Activate (la reina 👑) hasta marcos como SAP Application Extension Methodology, ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology), SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework y, por qué no… incluso SAP Business AI Framework.

Puedes preguntar sobre qué artefactos usar, cómo ejecutar los procesos o por qué tengo que hacer esta tarea... Y te diré lo que pasará si te los saltas... Muahaha! …💀 así que mis historias de terror, como el invierno… están por llegar.

🏛️ Cómocrear un SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) o brindar servicios de SAP AM (Application Management) y realmente vivir felices para siempre con SAP usando Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalización en SAP, ya sea de forma general o por país. ¡Larga vida a SAP Document & Reporting Compliance!

Y también a las extensiones DRC de nuestros partners SAP.

🧭 Roadmaps de soluciones y estrategia de transformación, etc

¿Y cualquier otra cosa? y ... ¡si no sé la respuesta! 👉 Prometo encontrar a alguien que sí la sepa… para ustedes.

 

Ya tenemos todo listo. 💬 Ahora es tu turno.

Deja tu pregunta en la sección de comentario👇 o puedes preguntar de forma anónima aquí 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions o escaneando el QR Code 

 

📅Esta edición del AMA estará abierta para preguntas hasta el 27 de marzo de 2026.

Después de esa fecha, las preguntas seleccionadas serán respondidas en un video grabado que se publicará en el canal de YouTube de la SAP Community  👉 @SAPCommunity

También se publicará un blog resumen con las preguntas y respuestas para la comunidad.

⚠️Solo se considerarán las preguntas enviadas dentro de esta ventana de tiempo.

 

Cuéntame…

🤓¿Qué tema de SAP te gustaría que exploremos juntos?  ESPAÑOL o INGLÉS.
